description: A Unity Energy audio series. Unity Faraday asks the questions. Clerk Maxwell — physicist, mathematician, and Chief Scientist of the Maxwellian Network — reveals the story behind the field.

episodes.title: The Electrogram
episodes.description: The opening conversation. Unity asks Clerk what the industrial electrical field actually is — and why, after more than a century of generating and billing for electricity, we have never truly understood what happens inside the field. The answer goes further back than you might expect.

episodes.title: Q&A — The Field Answers
episodes.description: A deeper session. Unity brings the questions engineers and facility managers actually ask: What is reactive energy, really? Why does the meter tell one story and the equipment tell another? Clerk answers from first principles — the physics that has been in place since 1865, finally applied to the 480-volt floor.

episodes.title: The Harmonic Story
episodes.description: Unity tells the harmonic story alone — what harmonics are, where they come from, and why they matter to every industrial facility that runs variable frequency drives, switching equipment, or non-linear loads. This is the invisible layer that every power bill hides.

episodes.title: Harmonic ROI — What the Cleanup Is Worth
episodes.description: The second part of the harmonic episode. Clerk and Unity work through the economics: what harmonic distortion actually costs a facility in heat, equipment stress, wasted capacity, and shortened transformer life — and what a harmonized field is worth in dollars per year.

episodes.title: Discovering the Unconducted Orchestra: The Value of Resonance
episodes.description: Clerk traces the idea of harmony from Pythagoras — who discovered mathematical ratios in vibrating strings 2,500 years ago — to the industrial electrical field, where every device has its own music and nobody has ever entered as a conductor. Until now. This is the philosophical foundation of why Unity Energy exists.
